name: F37 Analog
classification_notes: F37 frame Analog around late-1990s and early-2000s digital nostalgia, citing reference points such as the Game Boy Colour, Sydney 2000 and Y2K. Reads as a clean geometric sans with a slightly retro-tech accent.
summary: A geometric sans from F37 with a late-1990s and early-2000s tech accent, leaning into Millennium Dome and Y2K nostalgia.
description: A geometric sans from F37 Foundry that pulls on the visual memory of the late 1990s and early 2000s, from the Game Boy Colour and Sydney 2000 through to the Millennium Dome. Six upright weights run from Thin to Bold across a single width, and the foundry pairs it with F37 Hooj and F37 Blanka in their suggested combinations. Suits identity, editorial and packaging work that wants a clean Y2K-tinged voice without going full pastiche.
foundry_blurb: F37 present Analog through cultural reference points from the turn of the millennium, including the Millennium Dome, Game Boy Colour, Sydney 2000 and Y2K, framing the design as a contemporary geometric sans tied to that visual era.

name: F37 Aria
classification_notes: F37 anchor Aria to motorsport iconography, citing racing lines, Silverstone and the ARIA Music Awards, drawn as a tight three-weight display sans.
summary: A three-weight display sans from F37 with a motorsport accent, framed around racing lines and Silverstone.
description: A compact display sans from F37 Foundry built across three weights, Light, Regular and Bold. F37 pitch it through motorsport cues, with sample settings referencing racing lines, Silverstone and the ARIA Music Awards. Pairs in the foundry's suggestions with F37 Blanka Mono, F37 Randy and F37 Mancunio, sitting in editorial mastheads, sports and event branding where you want a sharp, kinetic sans with a defined voice.
foundry_blurb: F37 frame Aria around motorsport language, citing racing lines, Silverstone and the ARIA Music Awards, presenting it as a tight three-weight display sans.

name: F37 Atlantic
classification_notes: F37 present Atlantic with a transatlantic editorial reference set including Christopher Columbus, the Mayflower, Herb Lubalin and the Gulf Stream, drawn as a ten-style serif with matching italics.
summary: A ten-style transatlantic serif from F37 with editorial range, framed around the Gulf Stream, the Mayflower and Herb Lubalin.
description: A serif family from F37 Foundry across five weights with matching italics, from Light to Bold. F37 present it through transatlantic reference points, from Christopher Columbus and the Mayflower to Herb Lubalin and the Gulf Stream, suggesting an editorial and identity register. Pairs in the foundry's recommendations with F37 Qbik, F37 Neuro and F37 Bella Text, sitting in publishing, hospitality and brand systems that want a serif with both warmth and modern editorial bite.
foundry_blurb: F37 present Atlantic through transatlantic reference points, citing Christopher Columbus, the Mayflower, Herb Lubalin and the Gulf Stream, framing it as a ten-style editorial serif with italics.

name: F37 Attila
classification_notes: F37 describe Attila as inspired by the 1933 German typeface Krimhilde, presented alongside an Arabic companion, with a strong, slightly blackletter-inflected serif character.
summary: An F37 revival inspired by the 1933 German typeface Krimhilde, drawn as a six-weight serif with a matching Arabic.
description: A serif family from F37 Foundry inspired by Krimhilde, a 1933 German typeface, with six upright weights from Thin to Bold and a matching F37 Attila Arabic. F37 frame it through the figure of Attila the Hun and head sculpting, leaning into a powerful, historically rooted voice. Reach for it on editorial covers, posters, cultural and museum identities, and brand systems that want a serif with weight and a faintly martial accent.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Attila as inspired by the 1933 German typeface Krimhilde and named after Attila the Hun, presenting it as a six-weight serif with an Arabic companion.

name: F37 August
classification_notes: F37 present August through Paul Friedrich August Renner and his book on mechanised graphics, framing the design as a contemporary geometric sans rooted in the Futura lineage.
summary: A twelve-style geometric sans from F37 in the Paul Renner lineage, with italics and a modernist editorial voice.
description: A geometric sans from F37 Foundry across six weights with matching italics, framed around Paul Friedrich August Renner and his writing on mechanised graphics. The reference points place it in the Futura family tree, with a contemporary cut suited to identity, editorial and cultural work. F37 pair it with F37 Zagma Serif, F37 Blanka and F37 Britain Serif, supporting modernist layouts that want geometric clarity without the chill of pure Bauhaus.
foundry_blurb: F37 present August through Paul Friedrich August Renner, citing his early-twentieth-century writing on mechanised graphics and typography, framing the design as a twelve-style geometric sans with italics.

name: F37 Beckett
classification_notes: F37 describe Beckett as a homage to the British Ministry of Transport's 1933 alphabet used on pre-Worboys road signs, with later context around the Worboys Committee and Kinneir and Calvert.
summary: A fifteen-style sans from F37 that pays homage to the British Ministry of Transport's 1933 pre-Worboys road sign alphabet.
description: A sans family from F37 Foundry drawn as a homage to the British Ministry of Transport's 1933 alphabet used on pre-Worboys road signs, with seven weights and matching italics plus an Extras style. F37 set its historical context against the 1963 Worboys Committee and the later Kinneir and Calvert signage system, giving Beckett a sense of British vernacular and twentieth-century infrastructure. Pairs in the foundry's suggestions with F37 Caslon Text, F37 Ginger Arabic Soft and F37 Blanka, suiting editorial, transport, cultural and heritage brand work.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Beckett as a homage to the British Ministry of Transport's 1933 alphabet used on pre-Worboys road signs, placing the design alongside Jock Kinneir and Margaret Calvert's later British road sign work.

name: F37 Bella
classification_notes: F37 frame Bella as timelessly stylish, citing John Pistilli, Herb Lubalin and Jan Tschichold, with a high-contrast didone display character extended into Cyrillic and Greek scripts.
summary: F37's award-winning high-contrast didone display serif, drawn in the lineage of Pistilli, Lubalin and Tschichold.
description: A high-contrast didone display serif from F37 Foundry, drawn across four weights with matching italics and extended into Cyrillic and Greek scripts. F37 describe it through John Pistilli, Herb Lubalin and Jan Tschichold, citing Pistilli's lettering for Sudler and Hennessey and the 1964 Pistilli Roman cut. The wider Bella system spans the standard family alongside Bella Display, Bella Stencil and Bella Text, suiting fashion, fragrance, hospitality and editorial brand work where you want bygone glamour with a contemporary edge.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Bella as a timelessly stylish display serif that mixes bygone glamour with a contemporary aesthetic, citing John Pistilli, Herb Lubalin and Jan Tschichold and Pistilli's lettering work at Sudler and Hennessey.

name: F37 Bella Display
classification_notes: F37 present Bella Display as the display cut within the Bella system, sharing the high-contrast didone character of the family and extending into Greek and Cyrillic display companions.
summary: The display cut of F37 Bella, sharpening the high-contrast didone character of the family for headlines and covers.
description: A display cut within the F37 Bella system, drawn across four weights with matching italics. Bella Display sharpens the high-contrast didone character of the standard family for headlines, covers and posters, with Greek and Cyrillic Display companions sitting alongside it. F37 pair it with F37 Bella Text in their suggestions, supporting fashion, fragrance, hospitality and editorial systems that want a glamorous high-contrast display voice.
foundry_blurb: F37 present Bella Display as a display cut within the Bella system, sharing the family's reference set around John Pistilli, Herb Lubalin and Jan Tschichold and its bygone glamour.

name: F37 Bella Stencil
classification_notes: F37 frame Bella Stencil as the stencil cut within the Bella system, applying stencil breaks to the family's high-contrast didone forms.
summary: The stencil cut of F37 Bella, taking the family's high-contrast didone character into stencilled fashion and editorial territory.
description: A stencil cut within the F37 Bella system, drawn across four upright weights from Thin to Bold. Bella Stencil applies clean stencil breaks to the family's high-contrast didone forms, sitting alongside Bella, Bella Display and Bella Text in the wider system. F37 pair it with F37 Bella, F37 Blanka and F37 Zagma, supporting fashion, fragrance, hospitality, cultural and event branding where you want glamour with a slightly mechanical edge.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Bella Stencil through the same reference set as the wider Bella family, citing John Pistilli, Herb Lubalin and Jan Tschichold, presenting it as the stencil cut of the system.

name: F37 Bella Text
classification_notes: F37 present Bella Text as the text optical cut within the Bella system, softening the family's high contrast for body sizes and extending into Cyrillic and Greek companions.
summary: The text optical cut of F37 Bella, tuned for body sizes with matching Cyrillic and Greek companions.
description: A text optical cut within the F37 Bella system, drawn across four weights with matching italics and extended into Cyrillic and Greek text companions. Bella Text tempers the family's high-contrast didone character for running copy, decks and captions, sitting alongside Bella, Bella Display and Bella Stencil. F37 pair it with F37 Bella in their suggestions, supporting fashion, fragrance, hospitality and editorial systems that want both glamorous display and readable text from a single voice.
foundry_blurb: F37 present Bella Text through the same reference set as the wider Bella family, citing John Pistilli, Herb Lubalin and Jan Tschichold, framing it as the text cut tuned for body sizes.

name: F37 Bergman
classification_notes: F37 describe Bergman as designed by Hans Möhring in 1931, used by Ingmar Bergman in 1957 and revived by F37 in 2018, drawn across standard, narrow and condensed widths with an Arabic companion.
summary: F37's 2018 revival of Hans Möhring's 1931 typeface used by Ingmar Bergman in 1957, with thirty-six styles across three widths.
description: A serif family from F37 Foundry that revives a 1931 typeface by Hans Möhring, famously used by Ingmar Bergman in 1957 and brought back by F37 in 2018. Thirty-six styles run across six weights and three widths, from standard through narrow to condensed, with matching italics throughout and an F37 Bergman Arabic companion. The reference set sits in mid-twentieth-century European cinema and graphic design, supporting cultural identities, hospitality, editorial covers and brand systems that want a serif with a strong film and modernist accent.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Bergman as designed by Hans Möhring in 1931, used by Ingmar Bergman in 1957 and revived by F37 in 2018, framed alongside Bergman's films from Smiles of a Summer Night to Fanny and Alexander.

name: F37 Blanka
classification_notes: F37 describe Blanka as their take on a clean, classic Swiss sans, framed against the International Typographic Style, the Kunstgewerbeschule Zürich and the Basel School of Design.
summary: F37's take on a clean Swiss sans, drawn across forty-eight styles in three widths with one of the broadest script coverages in the catalogue.
description: A neo-grotesque sans from F37 Foundry presented as the foundry's take on a clean, classic Swiss sans, economical and very legible at small sizes. Forty-eight Latin styles run across eight weights and three widths, from standard through expanded to extended, with matching italics throughout and a companion mono cut. Script support stretches across Arabic, Cyrillic, Devanagari, Greek, Hebrew, Pan African and Thai, giving Blanka one of the broadest reach lists in the F37 catalogue and a natural fit for international branding, product, editorial and body-copy systems.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Blanka as their take on a clean, classic Swiss sans, perfect for body copy and economical at small sizes, set against the International Typographic Style, the Kunstgewerbeschule Zürich and the Basel School of Design.

name: F37 Blanka Mono
classification_notes: F37 present Blanka Mono as the monospaced companion to F37 Blanka, drawn in the same Swiss neo-grotesque idiom and tied to the International Typographic Style.
summary: The monospaced companion to F37 Blanka, drawn across sixteen styles with Cyrillic and Greek extensions.
description: A monospaced sans from F37 Foundry drawn as the companion to F37 Blanka, holding the family's clean Swiss neo-grotesque character on a fixed-width grid. Sixteen Latin styles run across eight weights with matching italics, with Cyrillic and Greek companions extending the reach. F37 pair it with F37 Britain Typewriter, F37 Britain Slab and F37 Hooj, suiting code samples, technical documentation, product UI and brand systems that want a calm, modernist monospace with a Swiss accent.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Blanka Mono through the same Swiss reference set as the wider Blanka family, citing the International Typographic Style, the Kunstgewerbeschule Zürich and the Basel School of Design, presenting it as the monospaced cut of the system.

name: F37 Bobby
classification_notes: F37 describe Bobby as a warm, friendly headline serif with a subtle geometric twist. Letterforms sit between a traditional serif and a geometric construction, giving display copy a soft personality without leaving the serif family.
summary: A warm, friendly headline serif with a subtle geometric twist, built for display sizes and brand voice.
description: A headline serif with a quietly geometric construction. Three weights with matching italics give it enough range for a tight identity system, and the Arabic companion extends it into bilingual work. Sits well in lifestyle, fashion and editorial branding where you want a serif that feels approachable rather than classical.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Bobby as a warm and friendly headline serif with a subtle geometric twist, and pair the typeface with a note about chess champion Bobby Fischer.

name: F37 Bolton
classification_notes: F37 describe Bolton as a brand sans with distinctive flat, perfectly horizontal ascenders and descenders, an unusual approach that gives it a strong typographic colour at display sizes.
summary: A brand sans with flat horizontal ascenders and descenders, ten weights deep and ranging from Hair to SuperBold.
description: A geometric sans built for identity work. Twenty styles run across ten weights from Hair to SuperBold with matching italics, and the family extends into Arabic, Cyrillic and Greek for multi-script systems. The defining detail is the cut-off ascenders and descenders, which give Bolton a tight, slab-like vertical rhythm without committing to a slab serif.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Bolton's distinctive characters as flat, perfectly horizontal ascenders and descenders, calling out the approach as unusual and pairing the typeface with a note about Bolton Wanderers Football Club.

name: F37 Britain Condensed
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Condensed as part of the F37 Britain collection, a set of seven characterful fonts that take their cue from British vernacular type. The Condensed cut is a tall, narrow display sans drawn with upright, oblique and backslanted variants.
summary: The condensed display cut in the F37 Britain collection, drawn with Regular, Oblique and Backslanted variants for poster and packaging work.
description: A condensed display sans that sits within F37 Britain, a seven-family collection rooted in British vernacular signage. Three styles cover an upright Regular, an Oblique and a Backslanted cut, all narrow enough for stacked posters, packaging strips and mastheads. Pairs naturally with the other Britain families when you want a coordinated typographic system with a deliberately British character.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Condensed with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Britain Humanist
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Humanist as the humanist sans within the F37 Britain collection. It carries warmer, more calligraphic proportions than the geometric Britain Sans cut and is designed to read comfortably from text up to headline sizes.
summary: The humanist sans within the F37 Britain collection, with calligraphic proportions across five weights from Hairline to Bold.
description: A humanist sans drawn for the F37 Britain collection. Ten styles run across five weights from Hairline to Bold with matching italics, so it can carry both running text and headline work. Sits alongside the other Britain families when you want a more open, calligraphic counterpart to the more neutral Britain Sans.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Humanist with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Britain Sans
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Sans as a neutral sans within the F37 Britain collection. It carries the standard, workhorse role in the system: a clean, mid-weight grotesque with a generous weight range.
summary: The workhorse sans within the F37 Britain collection, running from Light to Black with matching italics.
description: A neutral sans that anchors the F37 Britain collection. Ten styles span five weights from Light to Black with matching italics, giving it the range to handle body text, headlines and UI within a single system. Reads as a contemporary British grotesque, deliberately less expressive than the other Britain cuts so the more characterful styles can do the heavy lifting.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Sans with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Britain Scotch
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Scotch as a scotch roman within the F37 Britain collection. The style descends from nineteenth century British and Scottish foundries: high contrast, bracketed serifs, and a sturdy, editorial feel.
summary: The scotch roman within the F37 Britain collection, a high contrast serif with editorial weight across four cuts.
description: A scotch roman that joins the F37 Britain collection. Eight styles run across four weights from Regular to Bold with matching italics, giving it the range for editorial display, mastheads and book covers. Carries the historical British printing reference that the wider collection plays with, paired against the more neutral and humanist sans cuts.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Scotch with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Britain Serif
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Serif as a transitional serif within the F37 Britain collection. It sits between the Scotch and Slab cuts: bracketed serifs, moderate contrast, drawn to read at both text and display sizes.
summary: The transitional serif within the F37 Britain collection, running across four weights with matching italics.
description: A transitional serif drawn for the F37 Britain collection. Eight styles cover four weights from Light to Bold with matching italics, giving it room for book text and editorial display. Reads as a calmer, more workmanlike serif than Britain Scotch, useful when the Scotch's high contrast feels too dramatic.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Serif with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Britain Slab
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Slab as the slab cut within the F37 Britain collection. It draws on nineteenth century British wood type and signage with rectangular, unbracketed serifs.
summary: The slab serif within the F37 Britain collection, eight styles deep with matching italics for poster and editorial work.
description: A slab serif that joins the F37 Britain collection. Eight styles run across four weights from Regular to Black with matching italics, giving it enough weight range for both poster and editorial use. The shapes lean on British wood type and shopfront signage rather than the American Clarendon lineage, slotting next to the Scotch and Typewriter cuts in the wider system.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Slab with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Britain Typewriter
classification_notes: F37 classify F37 Britain Typewriter as the typewriter cut within the F37 Britain collection. It carries the mechanical, slabby skeleton of mid-century British typewriters but is drawn proportionally rather than as a fixed-width.
summary: The typewriter slab within the F37 Britain collection, six weights deep from ExtraLight to Bold with matching italics.
description: A typewriter style slab that closes out the F37 Britain collection. Twelve styles span six weights from ExtraLight to Bold with matching italics, giving it enough range for body text and headline use. Reads as a contemporary reworking of British typewriter type, useful when you want a slab with a mechanical, documentary feel rather than a Clarendon flourish.
foundry_blurb: F37 introduce the wider F37 Britain collection as a collection of seven colourful, characterful fonts that take their cue from a miscellany of Britishness, and pair Britain Typewriter with notes on Great Britain as an island and political union.

name: F37 Caligari
classification_notes: F37 describe F37 Caligari as Das Cabinet des Dr. Caligari but if it was made in 2024. It is a contemporary blackletter and textura display family, drawing on German Expressionist cinema for its sharp angles, twisted forms and high contrast.
summary: A contemporary blackletter display family inspired by The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari, six weights deep from Regular to Black.
description: A textura blackletter reworked for present day display use. Six weights run from Regular to Black, with sharp pointed forms and twisted, oblique lines that draw on German Expressionist cinema rather than ecclesiastical type. Sits in posters, record sleeves, fashion editorial and anywhere a gothic display voice needs to feel cinematic rather than antique.
foundry_blurb: F37 frame F37 Caligari as Das Cabinet des Dr. Caligari but if it was made in 2024, citing the 1920 German silent horror film, its Expressionist visual style of sharp-pointed forms and oblique, curving lines, and the directors' wartime distrust of authority as references for the typeface.

name: F37 Caslon
classification_notes: F37 describe F37 Caslon as their personal take on a stone-cold classic. The family is a contemporary reworking of William Caslon I's eighteenth century old-style serif, drawn with display proportions and a Condensed companion.
summary: F37's display reworking of William Caslon I's eighteenth century serif, with a Condensed companion and matching italics.
description: A display weight Caslon family of sixteen styles. Four weights run from Regular to ExtraBold with matching italics, and a Condensed cut runs across the same four weights and italics for tighter measures. Sits alongside F37 Caslon Text and F37 Caslon Hairline, which extend the system into smaller text settings and a hairline display register. Useful in fashion, editorial and luxury identity work where you want a recognisable Caslon voice without the dated contours of the original.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Caslon as their personal take on a stone-cold classic, designed by William Caslon I circa 1722, and credit Caslon as the English typefounder whose distinction and legibility secured him the patronage of leading printers in England and on the continent.

name: F37 Caslon Hairline
classification_notes: F37 position F37 Caslon Hairline as the hairline display cut within the F37 Caslon system. It carries extremely fine strokes designed for very large display use only.
summary: The hairline display cut of F37 Caslon, with very fine strokes for large display use across upright and Condensed widths.
description: A hairline display Caslon. Sixteen styles run across four weights with matching italics, plus a Condensed width with its own matching italics, all drawn at hairline contrast. Built strictly for very large sizes, the kind of magazine cover or fashion campaign type where the thin strokes can hold their weight. Pairs with F37 Caslon Text for body copy and the standard F37 Caslon for mid-display sizes.
foundry_blurb: F37 place F37 Caslon Hairline within the wider F37 Caslon system, citing William Caslon I's circa 1722 design and Caslon's reputation among the leading printers of his day in England and on the continent.

name: F37 Caslon Text
classification_notes: F37 position F37 Caslon Text as the text optical cut within the F37 Caslon system. Proportions, spacing and contrast are tuned for running body copy, with the same width and weight structure as the display Caslon.
summary: The text optical cut of F37 Caslon, tuned for running body copy across upright and Condensed widths.
description: A text optical Caslon. Sixteen styles span four weights from Regular to ExtraBold with matching italics, plus a Condensed cut with its own italics, all drawn for legibility at body sizes. Slightly heavier strokes, more open spacing and softer contrast than the standard F37 Caslon, which lets it carry long-form editorial and book settings. Sits in the middle of the F37 Caslon optical system, between F37 Caslon Text for body and F37 Caslon Hairline for very large display.
foundry_blurb: F37 place F37 Caslon Text within the wider F37 Caslon system, citing William Caslon I's circa 1722 design and Caslon's reputation among the leading printers of his day in England and on the continent.

name: F37 Drago
designer: Rick Banks, Rodrigo Fuenzalida, Ryan Williamson, Luke Charsley
classification_notes: F37 describes Drago as a modern serif with an ancient history, inspired by Columbus, a Hermann Ihlenburg titling face originally designed in 1892 and spotted by F37 in 1970s Jaguar press ads. The release introduces a tilted variable axis and standalone Tilted weights, with nods to 1960s display faces like Trooper Roman and Bernase Roman.
summary: A modern display serif revived from Hermann Ihlenburg's 1892 Columbus, drawn with a tilted axis and unusual ball terminals for distinctive headlines.
description: A modern serif by Rick Banks and team that revives Columbus, an 1892 titling face by German punch cutter Hermann Ihlenburg, after F37 spotted it in 1970s Jaguar press ads. F37 stripped out the old ornate flourishes and erratic terminals, kept the wonky g and other oddities, and added a tilted variable axis with separate Tilted weights. Sixteen styles run across four weights from Light to Bold with matching Tilted and italic cuts. Best for fashion, editorial and identity work that wants a serif with personality rather than the standard editorial polish.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Drago as a modern serif with an ancient history, inspired by Columbus, a font originally designed in 1892 by Hermann Ihlenburg. They first encountered Columbus in 1970s Jaguar press ads, then jettisoned the over-ornate flourishes and erratic serifs for a streamlined aesthetic, taking the wonky g and other letters as the starting point for a tilted variable axis and separate Tilted weights, with nods to 1960s fonts Trooper Roman and Bernase Roman.

name: F37 Elastica Sans
classification_notes: F37 describes Elastica Sans as an industrial grotesque with humanist features, drawn as half of a sans and serif pair on a shared skeleton, with eight widths from Expanded to Ultra Compressed and OpenType features including small caps, ligatures and stylistic alternates.
summary: An industrial grotesque sans with humanist warmth, drawn across eight widths from Expanded down to Ultra Compressed to flex from headline to micro masthead.
description: An industrial grotesque sans by Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks, designed as half of a sans and serif pair sharing the same underlying skeleton. Single weight across eight widths, from airy Expanded through Regular and Narrow to a migraine-inducing Ultra Compressed. Low stroke contrast and tight apertures suit bold, graphic uses; the expanded width shouts, the regular sits sensibly, and the narrower widths handle punchy headlines in tight measures. Small caps, ligatures and stylistic alternates included. Pairs interchangeably with F37 Elastica Serif.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Elastica Sans as an industrial grotesque with humanist features, designed by Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks and released in 2025. They pair it with F37 Elastica Serif on a shared skeleton, with eight widths from Expanded to Ultra Compressed, low stroke contrast and tight apertures for bold and graphic uses, and a large x-height for punchy headlines in tight spaces.

name: F37 Elastica Serif
classification_notes: F37 describes Elastica Serif as a bracketed slab serif with moderate contrast and indulgent ball terminals, drawn on the same skeleton as Elastica Sans, with eight widths from Expanded to Ultra Compressed and OpenType features including small caps, ligatures and stylistic alternates.
summary: A bracketed slab serif with ball terminals, sibling to Elastica Sans across eight widths from Expanded to Ultra Compressed.
description: A bracketed slab serif by Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks, sister to F37 Elastica Sans on a shared skeleton. Single weight across eight widths from Expanded through Regular down to Ultra Compressed. Moderate contrast and indulgent ball terminals add a more sophisticated voice than the utilitarian sans, and the narrower widths push how compressed a serif can get while still holding texture. Small caps, ligatures and stylistic alternates included. Designed to be used interchangeably with Elastica Sans within a single composition.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Elastica Serif as a bracketed slab serif with moderate contrast and indulgent ball terminals, designed by Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks and released in 2025. They frame it as the more sophisticated counterpart to F37 Elastica Sans, sharing the same underlying skeleton, with eight widths from Expanded to Ultra Compressed and distinctive textures in the narrower cuts.

name: F37 F51
designer: Leo Philip, Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand
classification_notes: F37 classifies F37 F51 as techno, drawn from Johnson Panas' flyer and ticket designs for Manchester's Fac51 The Haçienda nightclub, with five widths from Condensed to Expanded and several unusual stylistic variants.
summary: A wide, square, industrial techno sans drawn from Tony Panas' flyer lettering for The Haçienda, expanded by F37 across five widths and six weights.
description: A techno display sans by Leo Philip, Rick Banks and Shaqa Bovand, based on Johnson Panas' flyer and ticket designs for Manchester's Fac51 The Haçienda nightclub. Originally drawn by the late Tony Panas, F37 digitised and expanded the character set into a wide, square, industrial system that runs across six weights from Thin to Black in five widths from Condensed to Expanded. Several unusual stylistic variants add a contemporary twist. Reach for it on music, nightlife, sportswear and brand work that wants Manchester rave-era attitude.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 F51 as a font that tips its baseball cap to the flyers and tickets of Manchester's Fac51 The Haçienda nightclub, originally drawn by the late Tony Panas, digitised and expanded by F37 Foundry. They classify it as techno; wide, square and industrial looking, with a contemporary twist from several unusual stylistic variants.

name: F37 Ginger
designer: Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand, Ryan Williamson
classification_notes: F37 classifies Ginger as a geometric sans-serif with unusual near-circular counters, drawn under the influence of Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic, and used by Google, Pepsi and Barbour. Supports Latin Extended, Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic through sibling families.
summary: F37's bestselling geometric sans, drawn with near-circular counters in the lineage of Futura and Avant Garde Gothic, used by Google, Pepsi and Barbour.
description: A geometric sans by Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand and Ryan Williamson that has become F37's calling card since its 2013 release. Near-circular counters and clean geometric shapes make it a regular pick for logos and headlines, with adopters including Google, Pepsi and Barbour. Six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics in twelve styles, drawn under the influence of Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic. The wider family extends into Rounded, Soft, Mono, Mono Rounded and Mono Soft, plus Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic scripts.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Ginger as their best-selling geometric sans-serif, with unusual near-circular counters making it an ideal choice for logos and headlines. They cite the influence of Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic on its geometric slant, and list Google, Pepsi and Barbour among the brands using it. Over multiple versions since 2013 they have refined proportions, extended weights and styles, and added Soft, Rounded, Mono, Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic siblings.

name: F37 Ginger Mono
designer: Rick Banks, Romain Tronchin, Ryan Williamson
classification_notes: F37 frames Ginger Mono as the monospaced counterpart to F37 Ginger, reworking the geometric shapes and near-circular counters into a fixed-width grid, with the same Futura and Avant Garde Gothic lineage as the proportional family.
summary: The monospaced sibling to F37 Ginger, holding the family's near-circular counters and geometric voice on a fixed-width grid.
description: A geometric monospace by Rick Banks, Romain Tronchin and Ryan Williamson, reworking the F37 Ginger system onto a fixed-width grid. Twelve styles run across six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, keeping the near-circular counters and Futura plus Avant Garde Gothic DNA of the parent family. Sits in tech, fintech and editorial work that wants a calm, branded monospace that talks to the wider Ginger system, and connects to Mono Rounded and Mono Soft for softer voicings.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Ginger Mono as a monospaced counterpart to their bestselling F37 Ginger, reworking the geometric shapes and unusual near-circular counters into a fixed-width form that is equally flexible and iconic. They credit Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic as the typographic influences behind the original Ginger geometry.

name: F37 Ginger Mono Rounded
designer: Rick Banks, Romain Tronchin, Ryan Williamson
classification_notes: F37 frames Ginger Mono Rounded as a rounded cut of the Ginger Mono system, keeping the near-circular counters and geometric DNA on a fixed-width grid with softened terminals.
summary: A rounded cut of F37 Ginger Mono, softening the geometric monospace for warmer code, UI and data work.
description: A rounded geometric monospace by Rick Banks, Romain Tronchin and Ryan Williamson, sitting between F37 Ginger Mono and the softer Mono Soft variant. Twelve styles run across six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, keeping the near-circular counters and Futura plus Avant Garde Gothic lineage of the parent Ginger family but with terminals rounded for a friendlier voice. Useful in product, kids, wellness and brand work that wants the rigour of a monospace without the engineering chill.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Ginger Mono Rounded as a monospaced sibling to their bestselling F37 Ginger, reworking the geometric shapes and unusual near-circular counters into a fixed-width, rounded form. They cite Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic as the influences behind the original Ginger geometry.

name: F37 Ginger Mono Soft
designer: Rick Banks, Romain Tronchin, Ryan Williamson
classification_notes: F37 frames Ginger Mono Soft as the softest cut of the Ginger Mono system, with the family's near-circular counters and geometric construction tempered by softened corners and terminals.
summary: The softest cut of F37 Ginger Mono, tempering the geometric monospace into a friendly, approachable voice for product and consumer work.
description: A soft geometric monospace by Rick Banks, Romain Tronchin and Ryan Williamson, the softest of the three Ginger Mono cuts. Twelve styles span six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, holding the near-circular counters and Futura plus Avant Garde Gothic lineage of the wider Ginger system but with corners and terminals softened further than the Rounded variant. Sits in consumer tech, wellness and friendly brand systems that want a monospace voice without any hard edges.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Ginger Mono Soft as a monospaced version of their bestselling F37 Ginger, reworking the geometric shapes and near-circular counters into a softened fixed-width form. They cite Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic as the influences behind the original Ginger geometry.

name: F37 Ginger Rounded
designer: Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand, Ryan Williamson
classification_notes: F37 classifies Ginger Rounded as a rounded cut of the F37 Ginger system, keeping the geometric shapes and near-circular counters and softening the terminals. The wider Ginger family supports 252+ languages across Latin Extended, Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic.
summary: A rounded cut of F37 Ginger, keeping the near-circular geometry and softening terminals for friendlier logos and headlines.
description: A rounded geometric sans by Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand and Ryan Williamson, the rounded counterpart to F37 Ginger. Twelve styles run across six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, holding the near-circular counters and Futura plus Avant Garde Gothic DNA but with terminals rounded for a warmer voice. Through the wider Ginger system, it talks to Soft, Mono, Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic siblings, and is built for logos, headlines and packaging where you want geometry with a smile.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Ginger Rounded as a rounded version of their bestselling F37 Ginger, with geometric shapes and unusual near-circular counters making it an ideal choice for logos and headlines. They cite Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic as the influences behind the original Ginger, and note that the wider family supports 252+ languages, with version 5.0 adding Arabic script support drawn in a contemporary blend of Kufic and Naskh.

name: F37 Ginger Soft
designer: Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand, Ryan Williamson
classification_notes: F37 classifies Ginger Soft as the softest cut of the F37 Ginger system, holding the geometric shapes and near-circular counters but softening corners further than the Rounded variant. Sits within a wider family that supports 252+ languages across Latin Extended, Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic.
summary: The softest cut of F37 Ginger, easing the geometric counters into a friendlier voice for consumer and lifestyle brands.
description: A soft geometric sans by Rick Banks, Shaqa Bovand and Ryan Williamson, the softest variant in the F37 Ginger system. Twelve styles cover six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, keeping the near-circular counters and Futura plus Avant Garde Gothic lineage but softening corners further than the Rounded cut. Sits in consumer, wellness, kids and lifestyle work that wants the geometry of Ginger with even less edge, and connects to the wider Latin Extended, Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic Ginger system.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Ginger Soft as the softest cut of their bestselling F37 Ginger, with geometric shapes and unusual near-circular counters making it an ideal choice for logos and headlines. They cite Paul Renner's Futura and Herb Lubalin's Avant Garde Gothic as the influences behind the original Ginger, and note that the wider family supports 252+ languages, with version 5.0 adding Arabic script support drawn in a contemporary blend of Kufic and Naskh.

name: F37 Grotesc
designer: Anaïs Lievens, Ryan Williamson, Rick Banks
classification_notes: F37 describes Grotesc as a quirky grotesque inspired by the wonderful Pica Sans, with a huge overbite on the cap C that almost looks like an O. The rest of the design takes cues from Breite Grotesk, a late 19th-century German sans. Sits in display registers, with F37 Grotesc Text as a more legible text companion.
summary: A quirky display grotesque revived from Pica Sans, with an exaggerated overbite on the C and G and a companion Text cut for body copy.
description: A display grotesque by Anaïs Lievens, Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks, born from a serious case of overbite on Pica Sans, a quirky old grotesque F37 found in a specimen book. They exaggerated the overbite on caps C and G and lowercase c, g and s, and steadied the rest with cues from Breite Grotesk, a late 19th-century German sans. Fourteen styles run across seven weights from Thin to ExtraBold with matching italics. Best for editorial, music and culture work that wants a sans with a smirk; pair with F37 Grotesc Text for body copy.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Grotesc as the font born from a serious case of overbite, designed by Anaïs Lievens, Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks after finding Pica Sans in an old type specimen book. They applied its exaggerated overbite to letters like C, G, c, g and s, with the rest of the font taking inspiration from Breite Grotesk. F37 Grotesc comes in two versions, a display cut with the quirkiness turned up to eleven and a Text cut that is more legible and optically balanced.

name: F37 Grotesc Text
designer: Anaïs Lievens, Ryan Williamson, Rick Banks
classification_notes: F37 describes Grotesc Text as the more legible, optically balanced text cut of F37 Grotesc, with the overbite and quirkier letterforms toned down to work at body sizes. Sits alongside the display F37 Grotesc.
summary: The text cut of F37 Grotesc, optically balanced for body copy with the display family's overbite calmed down.
description: A text grotesque by Anaïs Lievens, Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks, the calmer optical sibling to F37 Grotesc. Fourteen styles span seven weights from Thin to ExtraBold with matching italics, keeping the Pica Sans and Breite Grotesk lineage of the display cut but with the overbite and quirkier letterforms dialled back for legibility at smaller sizes. Sits in editorial and brand systems where the display Grotesc handles headlines and the Text cut runs the article.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Grotesc Text as the more legible and optically balanced cut of F37 Grotesc, with the quirkiness and overbite of the display version toned down for body copy. The wider Grotesc system was designed by Anaïs Lievens, Ryan Williamson and Rick Banks, inspired by Pica Sans and Breite Grotesk.

name: F37 Gruffy
classification_notes: F37 sits Gruffy in their display catalogue as a soft, character-led sans by Leo Philip, drawn in a single optical width with full upright and italic coverage for friendly headline and packaging work.
summary: A soft, character-led display sans by Leo Philip, drawn across six weights with italics for warm headline and packaging work.
description: A soft display sans by Leo Philip, sitting in F37's character-led display catalogue. Twelve styles cover six weights from Light to Black with matching italics, drawn in a single width with soft terminals and friendly proportions. Pairs comfortably with F37 Drago, F37 Moon and the wider F37 display library for consumer, packaging, kids and lifestyle work that wants warmth without rounding everything off into a bubble.
foundry_blurb: F37 list F37 Gruffy in their display catalogue as a typeface by Leo Philip, released in 2020 by Face37 Ltd. The page presents twelve styles across six weights from Light to Black with matching italics, alongside the rest of the F37 display and text library.

name: F37 Gruffy Round
classification_notes: F37 present Gruffy Round as the rounded sister to F37 Gruffy, sharing the parent's curly tails, slightly over-exaggerated curves and angled terminals, with corners and stroke ends softened to a full round.
summary: The fully rounded cut of F37 Gruffy, keeping the family's curly tails and friendly mood while softening every terminal to a circle.
description: A rounded humanist sans by Rick Banks, drawn as the fully softened companion to F37 Gruffy. Six weights from Light to Black with matching italics keep the parent family's upward curly tails, slightly over-exaggerated curves and angled terminals, but trim every stroke end to a complete round. Suits friendly branding, children's products, packaging and identity systems that want warmth without losing a contemporary edge.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Gruffy as fun, friendly and distinctly modern, citing its upward curly tails, slightly over-exaggerated curves and angled terminals. F37 Gruffy Round shares those skeletons with the corners and stroke ends softened to a full round, and sits in the same family as F37 Gruffy and F37 Gruffy Soft.

name: F37 Gruffy Soft
classification_notes: F37 position Gruffy Soft between the standard Gruffy and the fully rounded Gruffy Round. Terminals are softened rather than fully rounded, keeping more of the parent's angled cuts while removing the sharpest corners.
summary: A softened cut of F37 Gruffy that sits between the sharp original and the fully rounded version, keeping a friendly mood without going fully bubbly.
description: A softened humanist sans by Rick Banks that sits as the middle width between F37 Gruffy and F37 Gruffy Round. Six weights from Light to Black with matching italics retain the family's curly tails and over-exaggerated curves, with the terminals lightly cushioned rather than fully circular. Useful for branding, packaging and editorial systems that want a friendlier face than the standard Gruffy but more structure than the Round.
foundry_blurb: F37 frame Gruffy as fun, friendly and distinctly modern, with curly tails, slightly over-exaggerated curves and angled terminals. F37 Gruffy Soft keeps those features and gently softens the corners, sitting between F37 Gruffy and F37 Gruffy Round in the same family.

name: F37 Haggler
classification_notes: F37 set Haggler against London market traders and street-sale energy, sample lines name Portobello Road, Rodney from Only Fools and Horses and shop signage. The drawing is a sturdy, slightly compressed grotesque with strong personality at heavy weights.
summary: A punchy compact grotesque pitched at London market traders and retail signage, where Black and Bold shout from a stall front.
description: A compact grotesque by Rick Banks, drawn with London street markets in mind. Twelve styles run from Regular to Black with matching italics. F37 set the family against shop signage, clearance offers and the cadence of a haggling trader, and pair it with F37 Zagma Mono for receipts and pricing details. Useful for retail identity, food markets, posters and any brand that wants a sturdy, slightly cheeky voice.
foundry_blurb: F37 pitch Haggler at the world of Portobello Road and London market stalls, referencing Rodney and Only Fools and Horses, fruit and veg signs, clearance offers and the rhythm of street selling. They recommend it paired with F37 Zagma Mono, F37 Qbik and F37 Kookie.

name: F37 Holborn
classification_notes: F37 reference Letraset 1970, Lincoln's Inn Fields and modernist design alongside Holborn. The drawing is a wide geometric sans with a long weight axis from Hairline to Black, scripted to read as playful and corporate at once.
summary: A wide geometric sans nodding to 1970s Letraset and London's Holborn, drawn to flex from corporate to playful across nine weights.
description: A wide geometric sans by Rick Banks, named for the Holborn area of London and rooted in the Letraset era of the 1970s. Eighteen styles run from Hairline to Black with matching italics, and the family extends across Arabic, Cyrillic and Greek scripts. F37 describe the tone as both playful and corporate, which makes it useful for editorial titling, identity systems, modernist-inflected branding and posters that need long horizontal letterforms.
foundry_blurb: F37 link Holborn to Lincoln's Inn Fields, Letraset 1970 and modernist design, and frame it as playful and corporate at once. The family extends into Arabic, Cyrillic and Greek companions, and they suggest pairing it with F37 Britain Typewriter, F37 Lemon and F37 Saint.

name: F37 Hooj
classification_notes: F37 describe Hooj as a shameless tribute to Paul Renner's Futura and the wider family of German geometric sans like Erbar, Kabel and Super Grotesk. Reference points include 1990s house label Hooj Choons, with sample lines like 'Cafe del Mar' and 'Three Drives - Greece 2000'.
summary: A geometric sans that tips its hat to Futura, Erbar and Kabel, then names itself after the Hooj Choons house label.
description: A geometric sans by Rick Banks that openly references Paul Renner's Futura along with Erbar, Kabel and Super Grotesk. Fourteen styles run from Thin to Black with matching italics, and a companion Arabic extends the system. The name nods to the 1990s house label Hooj Choons, and F37 frame the family around club records and Mediterranean nightlife. Suits identity for music, hospitality, fashion and editorial design that wants a clean Bauhaus-era geometry.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Hooj as a shameless tribute to Paul Renner's timeless Futura typeface, along with other famous German sans fonts like Erbar, Kabel and Super Grotesk. They tie the name to Hooj Choons, the house label founded by Alex Simons and Red Jerry, and pair it with F37 Caslon Text, F37 Grotesc Text and F37 Blanka.

name: F37 Hybrid
classification_notes: F37 call Hybrid an unusual display font that mixes slab serif and sans serif elements within the same letterforms. The drawing carries small slab cues on some terminals while remaining largely sans, giving a distinctive textural read.
summary: A display family that quietly mixes slab and sans details inside the same letters, giving headlines an off-register texture.
description: A display family by Rick Banks that blends slab serif and sans serif features inside the same skeletons. Twelve styles run from ExtraLight to Bold with matching italics, and a Cyrillic companion extends the system. The mixed cues read as subtly off, which makes the family well suited to identity, editorial covers and posters where you want a clean look with one feature that makes a viewer pause.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Hybrid as an unusual display font that subtly mixes typographic elements from slab serif and sans serif letterforms to create a distinctly different aesthetic. They suggest pairing it with F37 Blanka, F37 Lineca and F37 Neuro.

name: F37 Incise
classification_notes: F37 reference Linn Boyd Benton, the inventor of the pantograph, and frame Incise around precision engineering and drawing instruments. The drawing carries flared, gently incised terminals, sitting between a humanist sans and a glyphic.
summary: An eight-weight incised sans that nods to the Benton pantograph and precision drawing instruments.
description: An incised sans by Rick Banks, drawn with the heritage of precision engineering in mind and pointing to Linn Boyd Benton, inventor of the pantograph that shaped much of twentieth-century type. Eight upright weights run from Thin to Black, with an Arabic companion extending the system. The flared, gently chiselled terminals sit between a humanist sans and a glyphic, useful for cultural identity, editorial design, watchmaking, premium spirits and any brand that wants quiet authority.
foundry_blurb: F37 link Incise to William Stanley, a British inventor of drawing and surveying instruments, and to the wider story of the Benton pantograph. The drawing reads as sliced and precise, and F37 pair it with F37 Grotesc, F37 Blanka and F37 Lineca.

name: F37 Jagger
classification_notes: F37 describe Jagger as a marriage of clean lines with a little kick, naming Mick Jagger and the Rolling Stones as the spiritual reference. The drawing pairs clean sans skeletons with subtly flared, off-kilter terminals.
summary: A four-weight display sans that pairs clean modern skeletons with one or two quietly cocked details, named for Mick Jagger.
description: A humanist display sans by Rick Banks that F37 describe as a marriage of clean lines with a little kick. Eight styles span Thin, Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics. Used widely in editorial and identity work, with credits including Supple Studio, Typo Circle, Elsewhen and Dunlop. Suits magazine titling, music and culture branding, fashion and packaging where the brief asks for a contemporary sans with a hint of swagger.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Jagger as a perfect marriage of clean lines with a little kick for good measure, naming Sir Michael Philip Jagger of the Rolling Stones as the reference. They pair it with F37 Blanka, F37 Neuro and F37 Zagma, and credit work by Supple Studio, Typo Circle, Elsewhen and Dunlop.

name: F37 Jan
subcategory: hybrid display serif with ink traps
classification_notes: F37 describe Jan as a hybrid of two classic faces, featuring eccentric ink traps and specially designed geometric licks. The name and reference text point to Jan Tschichold and his work on Sabon and Penguin Books.
summary: A hybrid display serif with eccentric ink traps and geometric flicks, named for Jan Tschichold.
description: A display serif by Rick Banks, drawn as a hybrid of two classic faces with eccentric ink traps and specially designed geometric licks. Eight styles span Thin, Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics. The family is named for Jan Tschichold, the modernist who shaped twentieth-century typography through his work at Penguin Books and on Sabon. Credits include Syracuse International Film Festival, Something Good, Traffic Digital and GSK, and the family suits editorial, identity and packaging that wants serif elegance with a slight industrial edge.
foundry_blurb: F37 frame Jan around Jan Tschichold, born Leipzig 1902, citing his work on Penguin Books and the Sabon typeface. They describe the design itself as featuring eccentric ink traps, specially designed geometric licks, and a hybrid of two classic faces. They pair it with F37 Zagma, F37 Ginger Arabic Soft and F37 Gruffy.

name: F37 Judge
classification_notes: F37 describe Judge as a versatile, impactful geometric sans that really looks the part in XL sizes. The family extends across four widths from Compressed to Extended, designed to dominate at billboard scale.
summary: A wide-ranging geometric sans system across four widths and global scripts, drawn to dominate at XL sizes.
description: A geometric sans system by Rick Banks, built across four widths from Compressed to Extended with four weights from Thin to Bold and matching italics, for 32 Latin styles. The family extends across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew and Thai. F37 frame the design around poster scale and broadcast graphics, citing names like Cowell, Walsh and Osbourne to suggest the family's tabloid-strength voice. Suits identity for sport, broadcast, music and entertainment, plus signage and editorial at large sizes.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe Judge as a versatile, impactful geometric sans-serif that really looks the part in XL sizes, with names like Cowell, Walsh and Osbourne anchoring the sample setting. The family extends across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew and Thai, and F37 credit work by Matt Voyce, Homeless World Cup, Isodope and Pinkish Pods.

name: F37 K9
classification_notes: F37 frame K9 around dog hybrids, with sample lines naming Puggle, Dorgi, Chusky, Dalmador, Wauzer, Yorkipoo. The drawing is a stencil-influenced display sans with a touch of cut-edge bite.
summary: A stencil-edged display sans with a tight, punchy mood and a wide K9 family of soft, round, slab, wedge and serif siblings.
description: A display sans by Rick Banks with a stencil-influenced bite. Eight styles run from Light to Bold with matching italics. K9 anchors a wide system that includes K9 Round, K9 Serif, K9 Slab, K9 Soft and K9 Wedge, all sharing the same skeletal proportions. F37 frame the family around hybrid breeds and genetic matchmaking, which suits youth brands, music, sport, podcast identity and posters that want a tight, loud voice.
foundry_blurb: F37 set K9 against names like Puggle, Dorgi, Chusky, Dalmador, Wauzer and Yorkipoo, and call the design a genetic matchmaker, tight and loud. They pair it with F37 Tinker, F37 Caslon Text and F37 K9 Slab.

name: F37 K9 Round
classification_notes: F37 present K9 Round as the fully rounded cut in the K9 family, sharing the same skeletons as K9 but with corners and stencil cuts softened to circles.
summary: The fully rounded sibling in the K9 family, keeping the same skeletons as K9 but with every corner softened to a circle.
description: A rounded display sans by Rick Banks, drawn as the fully softened cut in the F37 K9 family. Eight styles run from Light to Bold with matching italics, all aligned with K9, K9 Soft, K9 Slab, K9 Wedge and K9 Serif at the same metrics. The rounded terminals make this the friendliest cut, useful for youth brands, music, podcast identity, packaging and signage that wants a warmer take on the K9 system.
foundry_blurb: F37 link the wider K9 family to hybrid dog breeds like Puggle, Dorgi, Chusky, Dalmador, Wauzer and Yorkipoo, with K9 Round positioned as the fully rounded sibling. They pair it with F37 Tinker, F37 Caslon and F37 K9 Serif.

name: F37 K9 Serif
classification_notes: F37 present K9 Serif as the serif cut in the K9 family, applying classic serif terminals to the K9 skeleton while keeping the family's stencil-influenced cuts.
summary: The serif cut in the K9 family, marrying classic serif terminals to the K9 skeleton.
description: A display serif by Rick Banks, drawn as the serif sibling within the F37 K9 family. Eight styles run from Light to Bold with matching italics, sharing skeletons with K9, K9 Round, K9 Soft, K9 Slab and K9 Wedge. Classic serif terminals sit on top of the family's stencil-influenced cuts, useful for editorial covers, music identity, posters and any system that wants a serif counterweight inside the K9 set.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe the wider K9 family as a hybrid set, comparing it to crossbred dogs like the Chusky, a Siberian Husky and Chow Chow mix. K9 Serif is the serif sibling in the system, paired with F37 Tinker, F37 Caslon and F37 K9 Soft.

name: F37 K9 Slab
classification_notes: Slab-serifed cut within the F37 K9 family, which F37 frame around the Chusky (Siberian Husky x Chow Chow) crossbreed metaphor of a hybrid character. K9 Slab gives the family rectangular, low-contrast slab terminations for editorial and identity use, with an italic at every weight.
summary: The slab-serif cut of F37 K9, an eight-style family that crosses a hybrid sans-meets-serif character for editorial and identity work.
description: F37 K9 Slab is the slab-serifed cut within F37's K9 system, a family F37 describe through the metaphor of a Chusky, the Siberian Husky and Chow Chow hybrid. It pairs with F37 K9 Soft and F37 K9 Wedge to make a three-way sibling family, each running across Light, Regular, Medium and Bold with matching italics. Use it where you want a low-contrast slab with a workhorse character and a clear connection to F37 Tinker, Caslon and the wider K9 group in editorial, branding and packaging.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 K9 Slab as one of three cuts in the K9 family, with eight styles across Light to Bold plus matching italics. They frame the family around the Chusky, a Siberian Husky and Chow Chow hybrid, and pair it on the page with F37 Tinker, F37 Caslon and F37 K9 Wedge.

name: F37 K9 Soft
classification_notes: Soft-terminal cut within the F37 K9 family. F37 frame K9 around the Chusky crossbreed metaphor; the Soft variant rounds the slab terminations for a warmer, friendlier feel while keeping the same eight-style structure as Slab and Wedge.
summary: The soft-terminal cut of F37 K9, eight styles that warm the slab into a friendlier serif for editorial and consumer brand work.
description: F37 K9 Soft softens the slab terminals of the wider K9 family for a friendlier, more approachable tone, while keeping the same skeleton as K9 Slab and K9 Wedge. Eight styles span Light, Regular, Medium and Bold with matching italics, and the family is built so designers can mix the three cuts within a single identity. Reach for it on consumer brands, food and lifestyle, or any editorial system where you want serif personality without sharp edges.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 K9 Soft as one of three cuts in the K9 family, with eight styles across Light to Bold plus matching italics. They tie the family to the Chusky, a Siberian Husky and Chow Chow hybrid, and present K9 Soft alongside K9 Slab on the foundry site.

name: F37 K9 Wedge
classification_notes: Wedge-serifed cut within the F37 K9 family. Where K9 Slab uses rectangular slabs and K9 Soft rounds them, K9 Wedge tapers the terminations into triangular wedges, giving the family a sharper, more incised cut.
summary: The wedge-serif cut of F37 K9, eight styles that sharpen the family into a more glyphic, incised voice for headlines and identity.
description: F37 K9 Wedge tapers the K9 family's serifs into wedges for a sharper, more incised character. The cut shares the K9 skeleton with K9 Slab and K9 Soft, so the three can work together inside one identity, and runs across Light to Bold with matching italics. F37 pair it with F37 Tinker, F37 Sonic and F37 Caslon, making it useful where you want serif structure with a confident, slightly editorial bite.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 K9 Wedge as one of three cuts in the K9 family, with eight styles across Light to Bold plus matching italics. They place it next to K9 Slab and K9 Soft in the foundry's hybrid Chusky framing, and pair it on the page with F37 Tinker and F37 Sonic.

name: F37 Kookie
classification_notes: F37 describe Kookie as soft, round and cuddly: a rounded display sans with no hard edges, framed playfully on the foundry site through a David Bowie reference. A twelve-style family with seven weights from Regular to Black, each with a matching italic.
summary: Rick Banks' soft, fully rounded display sans, marketed as the friendliest font imaginable for consumer, kids and lifestyle brands.
description: F37 Kookie is a soft, fully rounded display sans from Rick Banks at F37, sold by the foundry as the friendliest font imaginable, with no hard edges anywhere in the letterforms. Twelve styles run across Regular to Black with matching italics, giving enough weight range to push from gentle body copy into bold packaging and signage. Sits naturally in consumer, kids, food and lifestyle branding where you want warmth without dropping into a script.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Kookie as soft, round and cuddly, the friendliest font imaginable, with no hard edges whatsoever. They tie the launch playfully to a David Bowie page reference and sell it as a twelve-style rounded family from Regular to Black with matching italics.

name: F37 Lemon
classification_notes: F37 frame Lemon around citrus, juice and zest, presenting it as a fresh contemporary serif with eight styles from Regular to Bold and matching italics. The samples lean into food, recipe and editorial use cases.
summary: A bright contemporary serif from F37, eight styles tuned for food, lifestyle and editorial work where you want zest without nostalgia.
description: F37 Lemon is a contemporary serif from F37, sold under a citrus theme that nods to its bright, juicy character. Eight styles run from Regular through Medium, Semibold and Bold with matching italics, with F37 pairing it on the page with F37 Zagma Serif, F37 Ginger Soft and F37 Qbik. Reach for it on recipe books, food packaging, magazine editorial and lifestyle identities that need a serif with a modern lift rather than a heritage cue.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Lemon as an eight-style contemporary serif across Regular to Bold with matching italics, framed around the lemon's culinary and sensory associations. They pair it on the foundry site with F37 Zagma Serif, F37 Ginger Soft and F37 Qbik.

name: F37 Lineca
classification_notes: F37 describe Lineca as an experiment that introduces a straight horizontal line into every letterform, even the round ones, citing a Mondrian-style quote about lines, awareness and rhythm. A thirty-style family with fifteen weights from Hair10 to Black with matching italics, including Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic cuts.
summary: An experimental sans where every letter is built on a single horizontal line, with fifteen weights and Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic cuts.
description: F37 Lineca is one of F37's signature concept families, a sans where a straight horizontal line runs through every letterform, including the traditionally round ones. The foundry quote a Mondrian-style note about horizontal and vertical lines built with awareness and brought to harmony and rhythm. Thirty Latin styles run from Hair10 through Black with matching italics, and Lineca extends to Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic at the same depth, so it can carry an editorial or identity system with a strong, repeatable graphic device. F37 cite uses by Local, Forum, Literaturhaus and Omidyar Network.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Lineca as a thirty-style experimental sans built on a single horizontal line through every letterform. They frame the design around a Mondrian-leaning quote about horizontal and vertical lines in harmony, and list use cases including Local, Forum, Literaturhaus and Omidyar Network. The family extends to Lineca Arabic, Cyrillic and Greek at thirty styles each.

name: F37 Lothian
classification_notes: F37 frame Lothian around the Scottish Lothians and Edinburgh references such as Holyrood Palace, the Royal Mile and Robert Burns. A twelve-style high-contrast display serif with six weights from Hairline to ExtraBold and matching italics, sitting in the Scotch Roman tradition.
summary: A high-contrast Scotch-style display serif tied to Edinburgh and the Lothians, twelve styles from Hairline to ExtraBold with italics.
description: F37 Lothian is a high-contrast display serif rooted in the Scotch Roman tradition, with F37 framing the family around the Scottish Lothians and Edinburgh references including Holyrood Palace, the Royal Mile and Robert Burns. Twelve styles run across Hairline, Thin, Light, Regular, Bold and ExtraBold with matching italics, so it flexes from delicate magazine headlines to confident luxury logotypes. Reach for it on editorial, fashion, hospitality and any heritage-leaning identity that wants a Scotch voice with modern proportions.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Lothian as a twelve-style high-contrast display serif with six weights from Hairline to ExtraBold plus italics. They wrap the family in Scottish references including Holyrood Palace, the Royal Mile, gin, whisky, fudge and Robert Burns.

name: F37 Mancunio
classification_notes: F37 describe Mancunio as inspired by one of the last original wooden street signs in Manchester, named for the Roman fort of Mamucium. A large thirty-six style grotesque with nine weights from Thin to Black and matching italics, plus a full Condensed width at the same depth.
summary: A thirty-six style grotesque inspired by a surviving wooden street sign in Manchester, with full standard and condensed widths.
description: F37 Mancunio is a grotesque sans Rick Banks drew from one of the last original wooden street signs in Manchester, named for the Roman fort of Mamucium. The family runs to thirty-six styles, nine weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, and a Condensed width at the same depth, so it can carry an entire identity system from caption text to billboard. F37 use it on city-of-Manchester references and pair it with serifs like F37 Bella; sits naturally in editorial, music, sport and cultural-institution work where you want a confident British grotesque voice.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Mancunio as a thirty-six style grotesque across Thin to Black plus matching italics and a Condensed width, citing the Roman fort of Mamucium and Manchester references such as St Michael's Place, Market Street and the city's musical heritage.

name: F37 Maulstick
classification_notes: F37 describe Maulstick as a smooth, fluid font that pays subtle homage to the craft of signwriting, framing the design around traditional signwriters who set out a sign with chalk and write it freehand. A ten-style family with five weights from Light to Bold and matching italics.
summary: A smooth, fluid signpainter-style face that nods to traditional brush lettering, ten styles from Light to Bold with italics.
description: F37 Maulstick is Rick Banks' tribute to traditional signwriting, a smooth, fluid family that captures the rhythm of a brush in the hand without slipping into pastiche. F37 frame the design around signwriters who set out a sign with chalk and finish freehand, without relying on digital fonts. Ten styles run from Light to Bold with matching italics, sitting well on hospitality, food and drink, craft brands and any identity that wants the warmth of handpainted lettering with the discipline of a complete text family.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Maulstick as a smooth, fluid font that pays subtle homage to the craft of signwriting. They sell it as a ten-style family across Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold with matching italics, framed around traditional signwriters working in chalk and freehand.

name: F37 Moon
classification_notes: F37 describe Moon as having a big x-height for an ultra-clean, ultra-minimalist look, with short ascenders and descenders giving a compact, bold feel. A fourteen-style geometric sans with seven weights from Thin to Black plus matching italics, with Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic extensions.
summary: A clean, compact geometric sans with a big x-height and short ascenders, fourteen styles plus Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic.
description: F37 Moon is a geometric sans built on a big x-height and short ascenders and descenders, giving a compact, bold feel that F37 sell as ultra-clean and ultra-minimalist. Fourteen styles run across Thin, Light, Regular, Demi, Bold, ExtraBold and Black with matching italics, and the family extends into Moon Arabic, Cyrillic and Greek so it can anchor a multilingual identity. foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Moon as a fourteen-style geometric sans across Thin to Black with matching italics, citing its big x-height, short ascenders and descenders, and ultra-clean, ultra-minimalist character. The family extends into F37 Moon Arabic, Cyrillic and Greek.

name: F37 Neuro
classification_notes: F37 describe Neuro as inspired by neutral Swiss sans-serif fonts such as Akzidenz and Helvetica, with a British influence layered on top, citing a Karl Gerstner-style line about the grid as an art that takes practice. A twelve-style neo-grotesque with six weights from Thin to Bold plus matching italics, and an Arabic extension.
summary: A neutral Swiss-style neo-grotesque with a British accent, twelve Latin styles plus an Arabic cut, for editorial and identity systems.
description: F37 Neuro is a neo-grotesque sans Rick Banks draws from Akzidenz Grotesk and Helvetica, with what F37 describe as a British influence softening the Swiss neutrality. Twelve styles span Thin to Bold with matching italics, and the family extends into F37 Neuro Arabic at the same depth for multilingual systems. F37 cite uses including Football Type 2 and Taking Root, and pair it where you want a calm, neutral grotesque with a little more warmth than a textbook Swiss face.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Neuro as a twelve-style neo-grotesque inspired by Akzidenz and Helvetica with a British influence. They quote a Karl Gerstner-style note about the grid as an art that requires practice and list use cases including Football Type 2, CRNS, Taking Root and Divorce.

name: F37 Pythia
classification_notes: F37 describe Pythia as on the face of it a grotesque sans, but with unusual, whimsical letterforms that have other ideas. The family is framed around oracles, divination and the Pythia of Delphi. Ten styles across five weights from Light to Bold with matching italics.
summary: A grotesque sans with a wilder streak, ten styles whose unusual, whimsical letterforms give brands a calm base with character.
description: F37 Pythia presents as a grotesque sans, but several letterforms break with convention to give the family a quirkier, more whimsical voice. Ten styles run from Light to Bold with matching italics, and F37 pair it on the page with F37 Blanka, F37 Zagma and F37 Lineca. foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Pythia as on the face of it a grotesque sans, but with unusual, whimsical letterforms that have other ideas. They sell it as a ten-style family across Light to Bold plus matching italics, wrapping the design in references to oracles, divination, Cumæ and Sibyl.

name: F37 Qbik
classification_notes: F37 describe Qbik as a new slant on hardcore geometric sans, bold, powerful and uncompromising, although less so in its Rotalic form. A thirty-style family with five weights from Light to Black, full standard and condensed widths, and unusually a Rotalic (rotated) cut alongside the italic at every weight.
summary: A hardcore geometric sans across thirty styles with a distinctive Rotalic cut, condensed widths and weights up to Black.
description: F37 Qbik is Rick Banks' hardcore geometric sans, sold by F37 as bold, powerful and uncompromising. The family runs to thirty styles across five weights from Light to Black, with both standard and condensed widths and an italic plus a rotated Rotalic cut at every weight, an unusual feature that softens Qbik's edge when you want it. Reach for it on Swiss-style poster work, fashion, music and youth-focused identities, the foundry's own samples include 'KUNSTGEWERBESCHULE ZÜRICH' as a calling card.
foundry_blurb: F37 sell F37 Qbik as a thirty-style hardcore geometric sans across five weights from Light to Black, with full standard and condensed widths and a Rotalic cut alongside the italic at every weight. They describe it as bold, powerful and uncompromising in italic, and less so in Rotalic form.

name: F37 Randy
classification_notes: A characterful text serif from F37 with twelve styles spanning six weights and matching italics, pitched as a workhorse for editorial setting with personality in its details.

classification_notes: A digitisation of an old American letterpress face called Stoutheart, expanded by F37 into a twenty-style system that runs across five widths from Ultra Compressed to Expanded.

name: F37 Tinker
classification_notes: A humanist text sans built around legibility and accessibility, named after US academic Dr Miles Albert Tinker, a pioneer in the study of type legibility. Available in Latin, Cyrillic, Greek and Hebrew.
summary: A humanist text sans designed for legibility and accessibility, named after pioneering reading researcher Dr Miles Albert Tinker, with Cyrillic, Greek and Hebrew companions.

Eight Latin styles span four weights from Light to ExtraBold with matching italics, and the family extends into Cyrillic, Greek and Hebrew companions, making it a strong choice for accessible interfaces, public information, healthcare and education systems where reading conditions vary. Drawing is open and unfussy, with generous counters and clear differentiation between confusable characters.
foundry_blurb: F37 describe F37 Tinker as a hat tip to influential US author and academic Dr Miles Albert Tinker, a pioneer in the study of type legibility. classification_notes: A flared, incised serif inspired by Irish sculptor Michael Biggs' letter carvings on the Arbour Hill Memorial in Dublin, with Latin and Arabic coverage and a companion stencil cut.
summary: A flared incised serif inspired by Michael Biggs' carved letters on Dublin's Arbour Hill Memorial, with twelve Latin styles and an Arabic companion.
